description: Golden-file sentence-tokenizer corpus (BuildPlan.md Task 0.3; SPEC.md §6.5, §15.1, §15.9). Shared verbatim by the backend tokenizer's tests (Task 1D, backend/app/text/tokenize.py) and the frontend playback engine's tests (Task 2D) so both sides cover the same edge cases instead of independently guessing which abbreviations matter. Each case's `raw_text` is a stand-in for one paper's display_abstract (pre-normalization); `expected_sentences` is the correct abbreviation-aware split — a naive `'. '.split()` tokenizer will produce a different (wrong) count/boundaries for every case except `baseline_no_abbreviations` and `baseline_two_short_sentences`.

cases.note: 'Fig. 2' must not be treated as a sentence boundary.
cases.raw_text: Results are shown in Fig. 2. Sample sizes varied widely across the six included cohorts.
expected_sentences: Results are shown in Fig. 2.
Sample sizes varied widely across the six included cohorts.

cases.note: 'vs.' inside a comparison must not be treated as a sentence boundary.
cases.raw_text: Response rates were higher in the treatment arm vs. placebo. This difference was statistically significant (p < 0.01).
expected_sentences: Response rates were higher in the treatment arm vs. placebo.
This difference was statistically significant (p < 0.01).

cases.note: 'et al.' followed immediately by a lowercase continuation must not split, even though the following word starts a new clause.
cases.raw_text: As shown by Smith et al. in a prior cohort, early intervention improved outcomes. No such effect was observed here.
expected_sentences: As shown by Smith et al. in a prior cohort, early intervention improved outcomes.
No such effect was observed here.

cases.note: Both 'e.g.' and 'i.e.' appear in the same sentence and must not split it.
cases.raw_text: Several comorbidities, e.g. diabetes and hypertension, i.e. the most common in this cohort, were controlled for in the regression model. Age was also included as a covariate.
expected_sentences: Several comorbidities, e.g. diabetes and hypertension, i.e. the most common in this cohort, were controlled for in the regression model.
Age was also included as a covariate.

cases.note: Genus abbreviation ('E. coli') is a classic false sentence-boundary trap distinct from the generic abbreviation list.
cases.raw_text: Cultures were grown from E. coli isolates collected at three sites. Growth was measured at 24 and 48 hours.
expected_sentences: Cultures were grown from E. coli isolates collected at three sites.
Growth was measured at 24 and 48 hours.

cases.note: 'Dr.' as a title and 'approx.' as a measurement qualifier both appear; neither should split.
cases.raw_text: Dr. Alvarez led the trial, which enrolled approx. 200 participants across four sites. Recruitment took eighteen months.
expected_sentences: Dr. Alvarez led the trial, which enrolled approx. 200 participants across four sites.
Recruitment took eighteen months.

cases.note: Decimal points inside numbers (dosages, confidence intervals) must not be mistaken for sentence-ending periods.
cases.raw_text: The mean dose was 2.5 mg per kilogram, with a 95.5% confidence interval of 1.8 to 3.2. No serious adverse events were reported.
expected_sentences: The mean dose was 2.5 mg per kilogram, with a 95.5% confidence interval of 1.8 to 3.2.
No serious adverse events were reported.

cases.note: Stacks several abbreviation classes ('vs.', 'et al.', 'e.g.') inside a single sentence to check the tokenizer doesn't split on any of them.
cases.raw_text: Compared vs. the control group, and consistent with Lee et al., e.g. in similarly designed studies, the intervention group showed improved adherence. Follow-up continued for twelve months.
expected_sentences: Compared vs. the control group, and consistent with Lee et al., e.g. in similarly designed studies, the intervention group showed improved adherence.
Follow-up continued for twelve months.

cases.note: An abbreviation-like token ('et al.') that is followed by a genuinely new sentence (capitalized, unrelated clause) — the hard case: the tokenizer must still split here despite the trailing period belonging to a known abbreviation, because this is also where the sentence actually ends.
cases.raw_text: This finding replicates the results of Chen et al. The mechanism, however, remains unclear.
expected_sentences: This finding replicates the results of Chen et al.
The mechanism, however, remains unclear.

cases.note: Baseline control case: no abbreviations at all. A correct tokenizer must not under-split here either.
cases.raw_text: Twenty-two patients completed the full protocol. Three withdrew due to unrelated scheduling conflicts. No adverse events were attributed to the study drug.
expected_sentences: Twenty-two patients completed the full protocol.
Three withdrew due to unrelated scheduling conflicts.
No adverse events were attributed to the study drug.

cases.note: Minimal two-sentence baseline, no tricky punctuation.
cases.raw_text: The study was terminated early. Enrollment targets were not met.
expected_sentences: The study was terminated early.
Enrollment targets were not met.
